channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Former Syrian president Assad is video gaming in a luxury Moscow apartment: Report
item.description: Syria’s former President Bashar al-Assad, whose notoriously brutal rule over the country earned him the nickname “The Butcher,” was deposed in 2024 after years of bloody civil war.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Heard kids saying ‘6-7’? It’s so annoying that schools are banning it
item.description: The teaching profession requires a certain degree of patience. Particularly when students discover a new trend to latch onto and repeat at every given opportunity. The latest so-called “brain rot” phrase to flood the classroom: “6-7.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How to watch Belgium vs North Macedonia for *FREE*
item.description: Here's our quick guide on how to watch Belgium vs North Macedonia for *FREE* in the UEFA World Cup 2026 Qualifiers.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: G+D y sus socios Nexi y Capgemini desarrollarán la solución “offline“ del euro digital
item.description: El Banco Central Europeo (BCE) ha adjudicado a la alianza empresarial liderada por Giesecke+Devrient junto al proveedor de servicios de pago Nexi y Capgemini el desarrollo de una solución integral que permita a usuarios y comercios de toda Europa realizar pagos con el euro digital en modo offline.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UK watchdog targets Google's 'strategic' role in search ads and a competitive market
item.description: Britain’s antitrust watchdog is labeling Google a “strategic” player in the online search advertising market, paving the way for regulators to force the company to change its business practices to ensure more competition in that market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Opinion: Data centers must disclose how much water they’re consuming
item.description: As AI infrastructure rapidly expands across the US, the public needs to understand its environmental impact.
item.dc:creator: Irina Raicu, The Mercury News

item.title: ChatGPT wants to be the new operating system. Here’s why that should worry us
item.description: In a single day, OpenAI laid out the two pillars of its next empire. First, it signed a sweeping deal with Advanced Micro Devices to secure no less than 6 gigawatts of GPU compute, an agreement that could give it up to a 10% stake in AMD if certain milestones are met.
item.dc:creator: Enrique Dans, Fast Company

item.title: HP has an 'affordable' 5K curved ultrawide 49-inch monitor with a 5-megapixel pop-up webcam - it's just a shame about that VA panel
item.description: HP’s 49-inch curved 5K ultrawide monitor targets professionals needing workspace, conferencing tools, and trading flexibility in one affordable setup.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: NHTSA investigating Tesla self-driving software over traffic violations
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has launched a probe into Tesla’s self-driving software following several incidents in which the system caused cars to violate traffic laws.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google and Figma partner in bid to remake real-time design with Gemini 2.5
item.description: Creativity has always been governed by time—not just how long it takes to bring an idea to life, but how long a creator can stay “in flow.” Every designer knows the frustration of an idea hanging in digital limbo. But those pauses, once accepted as inevitable, are now starting to vanish.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: Dominion Voting Systems sold and renamed as Liberty Vote
item.description: Dominion Voting Systems, the voting technology company that was at the center of controversy and false stolen-election claims after President Trump’s 2020 loss, has been sold and will be renamed as Liberty Vote, its new ownership announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Tesla’s ‘full self-driving’ faces another federal safety probe
item.description: US auto safety regulators are investigating Tesla Inc. over incidents in which its vehicles drove through red lights and violated other traffic laws while using the company's partial-automation software.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Quantum computing – it’s still confusing, but now it’s mission-critical
item.description: "You're basically creating molecular circuits in the machine's memory versus connecting together a bunch of steps in a piece of software," said Bill Vass. The post Quantum computing – it’s still confusing, but now it’s mission-critical first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network
